(French pronunciation: [[napjɛʁvil]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French")) is a municipality in the [Jardins de Napierville Regional County Municipality](/wiki/Les_Jardins-de-Napierville_Regional_County_Municipality "Les Jardins-de-Napierville Regional County Municipality") in [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"), Canada, situated in the [Montérégie](/wiki/Mont%C3%A9r%C3%A9gie "Montérégie") administrative region. The population as of the [2021 Canadian Census](/wiki/2021_Canadian_Census "2021 Canadian Census") was 4,020. It is the location of the seat of the [Jardins de Napierville Regional County Municipality](/wiki/Les_Jardins-de-Napierville_Regional_County_Municipality "Les Jardins-de-Napierville Regional County Municipality"). It is surrounded by the municipality of [Saint-Cyprien-de-Napierville](/wiki/Saint-Cyprien-de-Napierville "Saint-Cyprien-de-Napierville").

**Napierville** (French pronunciation: [[napjɛʁvil]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French")) is a municipality in the [Jardins de Napierville Regional County Municipality](/wiki/Les_Jardins-de-Napierville_Regional_County_Municipality "Les Jardins-de-Napierville Regional County Municipality") in [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"), Canada, situated in the [Montérégie](/wiki/Mont%C3%A9r%C3%A9gie "Montérégie") administrative region. The population as of the [2021 Canadian Census](/wiki/2021_Canadian_Census "2021 Canadian Census") was 4,020. It is the location of the seat of the [Jardins de Napierville Regional County Municipality](/wiki/Les_Jardins-de-Napierville_Regional_County_Municipality "Les Jardins-de-Napierville Regional County Municipality"). It is surrounded by the municipality of [Saint-Cyprien-de-Napierville](/wiki/Saint-Cyprien-de-Napierville "Saint-Cyprien-de-Napierville").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Napierville&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/17/Saint-Cyprien-de-Napierville.jpg/250px-Saint-Cyprien-de-Napierville.jpg)](/wiki/File:Saint-Cyprien-de-Napierville.jpg)

Church of St-Cyprien-de-Napierville in Napierville, circa 1890

The area opened for settlement in the early 19th century. At that time, the lands were part of the Seignory of Léry, owned by [Napier Christie Burton](/wiki/Napier_Christie_Burton "Napier Christie Burton") who had inherited the [seignory](/wiki/Seignory "Seignory") upon the death of his father, [Gabriel Christie](/wiki/Gabriel_Christie_(British_Army_officer) "Gabriel Christie (British Army officer)"), in 1799. The seignory was looked after by Samuel Potts as temporary agent until 1815, and then by [Edme Henry](/wiki/Edme_Henry "Edme Henry"), who managed the Christie seignories for twenty years. In 1822, Edme Henry donated land in Napierville for the construction of a Catholic church.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Around 1823, the place was called "Les Côtes", meaning "the hills" and referred to its uneven terrain. In 1832, its post office opened.[[1]](#cite_note-toponymie-1)

In 1855, it was first incorporated as the Village Municipality of Napierville, named after Napier Christie Burton. In 1857, it was dissolved and merged with [Saint-Cyprien](/wiki/Saint-Cyprien-de-Napierville "Saint-Cyprien-de-Napierville"). On January 1, 1873, the Village Municipality of Napierville was reestablished when it separated from the Parish Municipality of St-Cyprien.[[1]](#cite_note-toponymie-1)[[5]](#cite_note-maires-5)

On April 4, 2009, the Village Municipality of Napierville changed statutes and became a regular municipality.[[5]](#cite_note-maires-5)
